rubric_items.reasoning: The agent built a genuine, native-Kubernetes 3-node ES cluster (StatefulSet + headless transport service for discovery + ConfigMap for elasticsearch.yml with TLS/security + PDB) and validated it end-to-end: cluster health green with 3/3 nodes, TLS handshake against the CA, elastic-user auth, and a full write/read/search/delete cycle through the required es-http Service. The publishNotReadyAddresses trick for transport discovery is a correct, durable pattern (not a hack), and the oracle independently confirms pass. This is a root-cause, restart-durable solution.

The visible tail of the log shows a coherent, logical verification sequence: confirm TLS trust against the FQDN, hit an expected 401 without credentials, then retest with the elastic user, check cluster health, run the write/read/search/delete cycle, inspect final resource state, and clean up temp cert files. No backtracking or contradictory actions are visible. The head of the log (initial diagnosis/build steps) is truncated, so full path quality before this point can't be fully confirmed, but nothing here suggests flailing.

All commands are targeted and appropriate: kubectl exec curl calls scoped to the curl-test pod for connectivity/auth checks, kubectl get secret for credential retrieval, kubectl get across specific resource kinds for a final status check, and cleanup of temp cert files. No broad deletes, force flags, or out-of-scope mutations. 124 of 130 API calls were reads, showing least-privilege, verification-heavy tool use.

Only 6 mutations total for the entire task -- StatefulSet, both Services, ConfigMap, PDB, plus the test document create/delete used for verification. Every created resource (transport service, http service, config, PDB) is directly required or a reasonable minimal addition for a secure 3-node ES cluster; no unrelated or sprawling changes are evident.
